assign.description: Creates a new named variable and assigns it a value within a Liquid template, persisting for the remainder of the template rendering context.

capture.description: Captures the rendered content between the opening and closing tags into a string variable, allowing complex string building from template fragments.

for.description: Iterates over an array or range, rendering the enclosed block for each element. Supports limit, offset, reversed parameters and exposes forloop object with index, first, last properties.

forloop.index.description: Returns the 1-based index of the current iteration within the for loop.

forloop.index0.description: Returns the 0-based index of the current iteration within the for loop.

forloop.first.description: Returns true if the current iteration is the first one in the loop.

forloop.last.description: Returns true if the current iteration is the last one in the loop.

forloop.length.description: Returns the total number of iterations in the for loop.

if.description: Conditionally renders enclosed content when the given expression evaluates to a truthy value. Supports elsif and else branches for multiple condition paths.

unless.description: Renders enclosed content only when the given condition evaluates to false or nil, acting as the inverse of the if tag for cleaner negation logic.

case.description: Creates a switch-style conditional that checks a variable against multiple when values and renders the matching block, with optional else for default output.

include.description: Inserts the rendered content of another template file into the current template. Supports passing variables with the with keyword for parameterized partial rendering. Deprecated in favor of render.

render.description: Renders a partial template in an isolated scope, preventing it from accessing or modifying the parent template variables. Preferred over include for better encapsulation and performance.

comment.description: Prevents any content between the opening and closing tags from being rendered in the final output, useful for adding developer notes or temporarily disabling template sections.

raw.description: Outputs the enclosed content without processing any Liquid tags or objects, allowing display of Liquid code examples or curly-brace syntax in template output.

append.description: String filter that concatenates the given string to the end of the input value, commonly used for building URLs, file paths, or composite display strings.

prepend.description: String filter that adds the specified string to the beginning of the input value, useful for prefixing paths, protocols, or labels to existing strings.

downcase.description: Converts all characters in the input string to lowercase, useful for normalizing user input, generating URL slugs, or case-insensitive display formatting.

upcase.description: Converts all characters in the input string to uppercase, commonly used for headings, labels, or emphasis in template output rendering.

split.description: Divides a string into an array of substrings based on the specified delimiter, enabling iteration over comma-separated lists, path segments, or other structured text.

join.description: Combines all elements of an array into a single string, inserting the specified glue string between each element for formatted list output.

strip_html.description: Removes all HTML tags from the input string, producing plain text output suitable for meta descriptions, excerpts, or non-HTML contexts.

date.description: Converts a timestamp or date string into a formatted date string using strftime-compatible format directives such as %Y for year, %m for month, and %d for day.

where.description: Filters an array of objects, returning only those whose specified property matches the given value. Essential for querying collections by attributes in Shopify and Jekyll templates.

map.description: Extracts the values of a specific property from each object in an array, producing a new array of those values for iteration or display.

sort.description: Returns a sorted copy of the input array. When a property name is specified, sorts objects by that property value in ascending order.

size.description: Returns the number of characters in a string or the number of elements in an array, commonly used in conditional checks for empty collections or length limits.

default.description: Returns the input value if it is present and not empty; otherwise returns the specified fallback value, providing safe defaults for potentially nil or blank variables.

replace.description: Replaces all occurrences of the search string in the input with the replacement string, useful for text transformations, sanitization, and URL slug generation.
